title: After-Hours Access Alerts

compliance.assurance_rationale: SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — after-hours physical access is one of the anomaly signals SWIFT users feed into monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of SWIFT CSP 6.4 (Logging and monitoring) — Splunk UC-15.3.2: After-Hours Access Alerts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-15.3.2' running on Access events with time-based rules, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: TSA SD02C III.A Cybersecurity plan — after-hours detection supports the pipeline cybersecurity plan when control-room facilities are in scope.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of TSA SD III.A (Cybersecurity plan) — Splunk UC-15.3.2: After-Hours Access Alerts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-15.3.2' running on Access events with time-based rules, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — after-hours physical access is one of the anomaly signals SWIFT users feed into monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of SWIFT CSP 6.4 — Splunk UC-15.3.2: After-Hours Access Alerts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-15.3.2' running on Access events with time-based rules, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Ingest **Access control system** granted-access events into `index=physical` as `sourcetype="access_control"` with Splunk-extracted fields **`badge_holder`**, **`badge_id`**, **`door`**, and **`result`** (plus `_time` aligned to the controller clock). Typical collection uses **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase app **4740**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) or a Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` TCP/UDP listener; when the head-end exposes HTTPS only, POST JSON to HEC `/services/collector/event` using the same field contract so the operational SPL can stay unchanged.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control" result="granted"
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hour < 6 OR hour > 22) AND NOT match(badge_holder, "NOC|Security|Facilities")
| table _time, badge_holder, door, badge_id
description: Flags granted physical-access events on data-center mantraps, suites, and cage doors when they occur outside the facility’s published business-hours window so security operations can reconcile the swipe against an approved after-hours work order.
value: After-hours entry is a common precursor to insider misuse, theft of equipment, or undetected tailgating; timely Splunk alerts shrink incident response time, improve evidence for auditors reviewing physical-access programs, and help prove that exceptions are reviewed rather than silently ignored.
implementation: Publish per-site quiet hours (for example 22:00–06:00 local) in a lookup or automatic lookup on `door`, maintain an allowlist of roles or cost centers permitted overnight (`NOC`, `Security`, `Facilities`), require after-hours access requests to write a correlating ticket or CSV flag the search can join, schedule the saved search every five minutes with `dispatch.earliest_time=-10m`, and archive fired results to your evidence index with door, badge, and approver metadata.
visualization: Primary **splunk.table** panel sorted by `_time` with columns `badge_holder`, `door`, `badge_id`; companion **splunk.column** chart of after-hours counts by `door`; **splunk.singlevalue** for distinct `badge_holder` in the alert window; optional heatmap (`timechart span=1h`) comparing overnight versus daytime grant volume per site.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Authentication data-model acceleration only after `props.conf` maps `badge_holder` to `user`, normalizes `action`/`result` into the Authentication semantic, and aliases reader or controller identity to `src`; expect high cardinality on `user`—scope acceleration to production indexers that hold `index=physical` and re-test summaries with a `-24h@h` `tstats` dry run before promoting to search-time defaults.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Forward data to Splunk Enterprise
Splunk Documentation — Create and manage scheduled alerts
Splunk Common Information Model — Authentication dataset

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Corporate NOC badge swipes** — Engineering staff with `NOC` in their display name legitimately badge in during outages; suppress with a structured `department` field or CSV allowlist keyed on `badge_id` when `match` on free-text names is too coarse. (2) **Holiday or maintenance blackouts** — Facilities keeps mantraps open during planned outages recorded in your change-management tool; join `change_window=active` markers (if ingested on another index) or pause the alert via a `maintenance_mode` KV store updated by the change desk. (3) **Visitor escort tail events** — Escorted guests may badge on the same reader minutes apart; differentiate using `credential_type=visitor` when the PACS emits it or require `stats count by badge_holder` thresholds >3 before paging. (4) **Syslog backlog replay** — After controller fail-over the **access-control integration service** may replay hours of historic grants; detect burst rows sharing identical `host`/`source` metadata and throttle using `bucket _time span=1m` anomaly baselines.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Confirm the **Access control system** exports grant/deny streams with stable door and cardholder keys; land them on `index=physical` with `sourcetype="access_control"` and verify `badge_holder`, `badge_id`, `door`, and `result` appear in Interactive Search (`-15m` smoke test).
• Install **Splunk Connect for Syslog** (Splunkbase **4740**) or an approved Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` `TCP/UDP` stanza from the integration guide `docs/implementation-guide.md`; document VLAN, TLS, and syslog facility mappings.
• RBAC: grant `physical_security_analyst` read/search on `index=physical`, isolate alert-edit rights to `secops_scheduler`, and block dashboard tokens from storing API secrets.
• Validate time zones: `_time` must reflect facility local wall clock because hour buckets (`strftime`) gate after-hours logic.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Forward syslog from the access-control head-end or middleware to SC4S/the forwarder; alternatively configure vendor HTTPS export through HEC `/services/collector/event` with identical sourcetype stamping.
• In `props.conf`, preserve `TIME_PREFIX`/`TIME_FORMAT` from the vendor feed so `_time` matches door-controller telemetry within one second.
• Maintain `facility_hours.csv` (columns: `site_id`, `quiet_start_hour`, `quiet_end_hour`) and optionally `after_hours_approved.csv` keyed by `badge_id` or change ticket id for lookups joined inside alerts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
• Paste the catalogue SPL into Search, tune quiet-hour predicates to match lookups rather than literals when multi-site, then save as **Saved search `UC-15.3.2`**.
• Schedule `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`, set `dispatch.earliest_time=-10m`, enable **alert.suppress** on `badge_holder` and `door` for ≥30 minutes, and route notifications through your SOC queue.
• Optional enrichment: `lookup facility_hours.csv door OUTPUTNEW site_id quiet_start_hour` before evaluating hour windows.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control" result="granted"
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hour < 6 OR hour > 22) AND NOT match(badge_holder, "NOC|Security|Facilities")
| table _time, badge_holder, door, badge_id
```

Understanding this SPL
• **`index=physical`** scopes facility telemetry away from enterprise application indexes.
• **`sourcetype="access_control"`** binds to the Access control parsing contract documented in Data sources.
• **`result="granted"`** focuses on successful swipes—denied attempts are a different control.
• **`strftime` + `where`** encode the quiet-hour band; swap numeric literals for lookup-driven bounds per site.
• **`match` on badge_holder`** implements the operational allowlist until a structured role field exists.

Step 3 — Validate
• Execute three controlled swipes on a staging reader inside and outside quiet hours; confirm Splunk rows align with the PACS operator console for the same `_time`, `door`, and `badge_id`.
• Compare alert volume against a `-30d` baseline; investigate spikes tied to daylight-saving changes or controller fail-over backlog.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Place the table on a Physical Security dashboard with drilldowns on `badge_holder` and `door`, link to visitor/contractor ticketing, and document escalations when no matching work order exists.
• Retain PDF/CSV exports plus the Saved Search Job ID in your evidence repository; note owner, review cadence, and tuning history in the runbook.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During a tabletop exercise, facilities uses a staging mantrap reader to badge a test holder at 23:15 local while the Access control system streams `result=granted` rows into `index=physical`; within ten minutes the scheduled UC-15.3.2 search returns `door=LAB-MANTRAP-02`, `badge_holder` matching the seeded identity, and `badge_id` aligned with the PACS screenshot captured by QA.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A contractor badges successfully at 14:30 local during normal business hours on `door=SUITE-A`; the saved search returns zero rows because `hour` sits outside the configured `<6 OR >22` predicate and no suppression logic fires—proving quiet-hour logic does not page on daytime grants.

evidence: Retain Saved Search `UC-15.3.2` Job IDs or exported CSV snapshots showing `_time`, `badge_holder`, `door`, and `badge_id` for each after-hours grant plus links to the approving ticket or lookup revision referenced in the alert drilldown.
exclusions: Does not certify reader wiring, tamper alarms, camera correlation, logical IAM sessions, or vendor-native anti-tailgating analytics unless those feeds land in this sourcetype; does not replace the access-control database as the authoritative badge-status record.
equipmentModels: Physical access control head-end exporting syslog or HTTPS JSON to Splunk
Door-controller supervised mantrap reader infrastructure referenced by `door` metadata
